strategy_description: Velvet spike playbook: Frankfurt IV scalp on all VEV strikes with extract |ΔS|/z burst (lower THR_OPEN + IV_SCALPING_THR) vs calm (tighter). After calibration, IV_SCALPING_THR lowered from 0.7 to tape-aware ~0.37–0.45. TTE: state.csv_day + plot_iv_smile_round3 intraday wind (DTE 8/7/6 by csv day per round3description + combined_analysis).
Grid: BURST_OPEN_MULT=0.65 vs v0=0.72; same IV threshold scaling. Performance tied with v0 on this tape.
ATM cluster filter |K/S-1| ≤ 0.10 calm / 0.24 burst (widen on spike); same IV/open scaling. Avoids deep ITM wings where global smile misprices.
Grid: tighter calm (CALM_OPEN_MULT=1.05, ATM_REL_CALM=0.08). Matched v2 total on this run.
Grid: wider burst cluster only ATM_REL_BURST=0.26 (calm 0.10). Best aggregate; more VEV_4000 participation on day 2.

Conservative burst participation: higher burst THR_OPEN/IV gate, calm core smile MM retained, plus low-z calm inventory-flattening mean-revert fallback. Maintains ATM cluster widen-on-burst / pull-wings thesis.

v5 + grid: ATM_REL_BURST=0.22 and burst BURST_MAX_LOT=80. Tight burst band excludes VEV_4000 on tape (|4000/S-1|>0.22 for all ticks on days 0-2) — misfit vs widen-on-burst. Total regressed to 315 vs v5 433; documents that pulling deep ITM from bursts hurts more than it fixes day-0 PnL.
v5 + burst-only order size cap (BURST_MAX_LOT=80); same ATM 0.24/0.10 and conservative burst thresholds. Reduces day-0 drag vs v5 while keeping VEV_4000 in cluster on S~5.2k days.

v5 with two-tier burst size caps: 80 in calm_core band (|K/S-1|<=0.10) vs 40 in outer burst ring (0.10-0.24), from burst BS vega ~20x higher in core than ring on tape sample. Ties v7 PnL 487; prefer v7 (simpler) on tie.

Grid on best parent (v7): burst BURST_MAX_LOT=60 vs v7=80; else identical. Total 487 (ties v7); per-day PnL unchanged on this run — cap coarser than effective fill sizes or burst branch rarely hits max.
